content: read reviews/127.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/127.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/127.md.

    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (excess of max bipartite subgraph over Edwards bound; is f(m) unbounded?).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entries included: [Ed73] Edwards (1973), [Al96] Alon (1996).
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos127 ... end Erdos127` (lines 35–96).
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos127`.
    25→- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open Real Filter` at line 33.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] Helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`): `cutSize` (line 37), `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize` (line 49), `edwardsLB` (line 58), `f` (line 65).
    30→- [x] Definitions involving classical choice / nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`: all four definitions are `noncomputable`.
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    32→- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `cutSize`, `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize`, `edwardsLB`, `f`.
    33→- N/A: No custom types or Props defined (no `UpperCamelCase` check needed).
    34→- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    35→- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).
    36→
    37→## 6. Theorem Statements
    38→
    39→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 91).
    40→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_127` (snake_case matching namespace number).
    41→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
    42→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 90).
    43→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 79–89).
    44→- [x] Docstring references source papers: [Ed73], [Al96].
    45→
    46→### `answer()` usage
    47→
    48→The original problem asks: *"Is f(m) unbounded?"* — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    49→
    50→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(True) ↔ P`: correct form for a yes/no question.
    51→- [x] Since the problem was solved affirmatively by Alon [Al96], `answer(True)` is correct (not `answer(sorry)`).
    52→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: unboundedness of f is expressed as the existence of a strictly monotone sequence `seq` with `f(seq(i)) → ∞`. This correctly captures "unbounded" (along a subsequence, consistent with Alon's result that f(n²/2) ≫ n^{1/2}, rather than requiring f → ∞ everywhere).
    53→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(True)`, not as theorem parameters.
    54→
    55→## 7. Category Attribute
    56→
    57→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
    58→- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (solved by Alon but not formally verified).
    59→- [x] Consistent: `research solved` with `sorry` in proof body is correct (solved but proof not formalized).
    60→
    61→## 8. AMS Attribute
    62→
    63→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
    64→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
    65→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number, trivially satisfied).
    66→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    67→- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    68→
    69→## 9. Proof Bodies
    70→
    71→- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (lines 93–94) — acceptable per checklist.
    72→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    73→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved problem.
    74→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    75→
    76→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    77→
    78→- N/A: No variants present. The file contains only the main theorem `erdos_127`.
    79→
    80→## 11. Code Quality
    81→
    82→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    83→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∃`, `→`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∈`, `∉`, `∧`, `ℂ` not needed.
    84→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    85→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    86→- [x] Lines reasonably short (all <100 characters).
    87→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    88→

    90→
    91→- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.

    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Summary
    97→
    98→**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)
    99→
   100→The file is clean and well-structured. All checklist items are satisfied. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question ("Is f(m) unbounded?"), uses `answer(True)` since Alon solved it affirmatively, and the RHS properly captures unboundedness via a strictly monotone subsequence with f diverging to infinity. The four helper definitions (`cutSize`, `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize`, `edwardsLB`, `f`) are all appropriately marked `noncomputable` and have clear docstrings. Bibliography references are consistent between the module docstring and theorem docstring.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/127.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with heading: `# Erdős Problem 127`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/127](https://www.erdosproblems.com/127)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (excess of max bipartite subgraph over Edwards bound; is f(m) unbounded?).
- [x] Bibliography entries included: [Ed73] Edwards (1973), [Al96] Alon (1996).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos127 ... end Erdos127` (lines 35–96).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos127`.
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open Real Filter` at line 33.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`): `cutSize` (line 37), `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize` (line 49), `edwardsLB` (line 58), `f` (line 65).
- [x] Definitions involving classical choice / nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`: all four definitions are `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `cutSize`, `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize`, `edwardsLB`, `f`.
- N/A: No custom types or Props defined (no `UpperCamelCase` check needed).
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 91).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_127` (snake_case matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 90).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 79–89).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: [Ed73], [Al96].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks: *"Is f(m) unbounded?"* — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(True) ↔ P`: correct form for a yes/no question.
- [x] Since the problem was solved affirmatively by Alon [Al96], `answer(True)` is correct (not `answer(sorry)`).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: unboundedness of f is expressed as the existence of a strictly monotone sequence `seq` with `f(seq(i)) → ∞`. This correctly captures "unbounded" (along a subsequence, consistent with Alon's result that f(n²/2) ≫ n^{1/2}, rather than requiring f → ∞ everywhere).
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(True)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (solved by Alon but not formally verified).
- [x] Consistent: `research solved` with `sorry` in proof body is correct (solved but proof not formalized).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (lines 93–94) — acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The file contains only the main theorem `erdos_127`.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∃`, `→`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∈`, `∉`, `∧`, `ℂ` not needed.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines reasonably short (all <100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All checklist items are satisfied. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question ("Is f(m) unbounded?"), uses `answer(True)` since Alon solved it affirmatively, and the RHS properly captures unboundedness via a strictly monotone subsequence with f diverging to infinity. The four helper definitions (`cutSize`, `maxBipartiteSubgraphSize`, `edwardsLB`, `f`) are all appropriately marked `noncomputable` and have clear docstrings. Bibliography references are consistent between the module docstring and theorem docstring.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
